title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Central Asian origin
description: Hindu Kush / Pamir region — Cannabis sativa + indica evolved here. Earliest archaeological evidence of ritual cannabis use (~500 BCE, Pazyryk burials).

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Central Asian origin
description: Persia — Zoroastrian and Scythian ritual use; Herodotus 'Histories' (440 BCE) documents Scythian hemp-smoke bathing.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Silk Road + Middle Eastern spread
description: Afghanistan — hashish production ancestor to modern Middle Eastern + North African concentrates.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Silk Road + Middle Eastern spread
description: Morocco Rif Mountains — 15th-century hashish production; 19th century French colonial formalization.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Silk Road + Middle Eastern spread
description: India — bhang documented in Atharva Veda (~1500 BCE); Shiva lore. Cannabis sacramental through Hindu tradition.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Colonial + enslaved diffusion to Americas
description: Caribbean — enslaved Africans + indentured Indians bring cannabis culture to plantations 1800s.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Colonial + enslaved diffusion to Americas
description: Jamaica — Indian indentured labour (post-1838 abolition) seeds Rastafari cannabis sacrament. Ganja becomes central religious practice.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Colonial + enslaved diffusion to Americas
description: Mexico — cannabis arrives via Spanish hemp imports; becomes associated with Mexican labourers in early 20th-century US racial panic.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: US Prohibition + global drug war
description: Washington DC — 1937 Marihuana Tax Act (Harry Anslinger). 1970 Controlled Substances Act Schedule I.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: US Prohibition + global drug war
description: Indonesia — Dutch colonial + later US-influenced criminalization; death penalty for trafficking.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: US Prohibition + global drug war
description: US South — drug war enforcement falls disproportionately on Black + Latino communities (Alexander 'The New Jim Crow' 2010).

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Modern legalization wave
description: Prague — Czechia decriminalized 2010.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Modern legalization wave
description: Ottawa — Canada legalized recreational cannabis nationally in 2018.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Modern legalization wave
description: California — 1996 Prop 215 first US medical legalization. 2016 Prop 64 recreational.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Modern legalization wave
description: Berlin — Germany legalized recreational cannabis in 2024.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: cannabis / ganja / weed
description: Sanskrit — 'bhanga' (बङ्ग). Ancient root of 'bhang' drinkable cannabis preparation.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: cannabis / ganja / weed
description: Jamaican Patois — 'ganja' (from Sanskrit + Hindi 'ganja' meaning hemp resin).

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: cannabis / ganja / weed
description: Arabic — 'ḥashīsh' meaning 'grass/herb'. Source of English 'hashish'.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: cannabis / ganja / weed
description: Latin — 'cannabis' (from Greek 'kannabis', from proto-Iranian). Global scientific name.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Afghanistan — still a major illicit hashish producer.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Morocco Rif — largest hashish exporter to Europe.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: South America — Paraguay is major illicit supplier to Brazil + Southern Cone.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: California — legal market ~$5B/year; Mexican cartels still control illicit supply across border.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Canada — legal market ~$4B/year, exports to Germany + Israel + Australia.
